Title: Maize RNA 3’-terminal phosphate cyclase-like protein promotes 18S pre-rRNA cleavage and is important for kernel development

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W22 (Mutator line UFMu-02204 allele); B73 and C01 backgrounds used in validation/expression analyses
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: UFMu-02204/W22-derived mutant; B73; C01 CRISPR lines
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RNA 3'-terminal phosphate cyclase-like (RCL) protein; contains one long RNA 3'-terminal cyclase domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in maize tissues; relatively high in leaf, root, shoot; during seed development enriched in embryo and aleurone, with slightly higher expression in 10 DAP endosperm and 20 DAP embryo
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in root, leaf, stem, tassel, anther, husk, ear, and seed; strong in embryo cells and aleurone at 5–20 DAP
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: starch; zein storage proteins; free amino acids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm (starch granules and protein bodies of developing/mature kernels)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage reserves supporting kernel filling, seed weight, and nutrition during germination
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes storage reserve accumulation during kernel development; loss of RCL1 reduces starch per kernel and amylose, delays and lowers zein accumulation, and increases many free amino acids due to impaired translation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RCL1 is the causal gene underlying the lethal rcl1 kernel mutant and encodes a conserved nucleolar RCL protein. 2. RCL1 is required for 18S pre-rRNA processing, mature 18S rRNA accumulation, 40S/80S/polysome formation, and thus ribosome function. 3. Loss of RCL1 decreases translation efficiency of key storage-reserve regulators and enzymes, causing reduced zein and starch accumulation and defective kernel development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; Mu insertion co-segregation; CRISPR/Cas9 alleles; allelism test; genetic complementation with Ubi::FLAG-RCL1; RT-qPCR; in situ hybridization; subcellular localization; RNA gel blot; circular RT-PCR; polysome profiling; immunoblotting; starch/protein/amino acid measurements
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtRRP7 (co-localization marker only; direct interaction not shown)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for complementation; 35S promoter (constitutive) for localization assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Kernel developmental stage (5–30 DAP) is central to phenotype interpretation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes embryo lethality, aborted embryo differentiation, defective BETL and aleurone development, shrunken opaque kernels, and strongly reduced kernel weight
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a practical knockout target because loss causes lethality; preserving RCL1 function is essential in breeding. Could be considered as a housekeeping target to avoid deleterious edits affecting kernel filling and seed viability.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Referenced public RNA-seq gene atlas data for maize expression (Chen et al., 2014; Walley et al., 2016); no accession number reported in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RCL1 acts upstream of nutrient accumulation indirectly through ribosome biogenesis rather than as a metabolic enzyme or TF. The paper shows effects on zein and starch mainly via impaired transcriptional/translational capacity, especially loss of O2 protein and reduced abundance of starch enzymes.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT and rcl1 kernels (15 DAP); backgrounds include W22-derived mutant/B73 analyses
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major nitrogen storage proteins contributing to kernel nutritional reserves and vitreous endosperm texture
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 19-kDa and 22-kDa α-zein genes; also reported in paper discussion to activate PPDK1; PPDK2; SS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates zein gene expression; in this study O2 transcript increased but O2 protein became undetectable in rcl1, coinciding with reduced zein transcripts and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced O2 protein abundance in rcl1 is associated with decreased zein gene transcription and reduced zein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Predominant regulator of zein transcription in maize endosperm, used here as the key translationally affected regulator linking RCL1 to storage protein deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O2 mRNA is significantly upregulated in rcl1 endosperm. 2. Despite higher transcript levels, O2 protein is undetectable in rcl1, indicating impaired translation or protein accumulation. 3. Reduced O2 protein is consistent with downregulated zein genes and lower zein storage protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; immunoblotting of O2 protein; correlation with zein transcript/protein measurements
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: O2 is associated with OHP proteins in zein regulation context, but complex formation was not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: RCL1-dependent ribosome biogenesis/translation capacity during endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endosperm-associated regulator as analyzed at 15 DAP in kernels
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript assayed in 15 DAP endosperm; protein assayed in non-zein fraction from 15 DAP kernels
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of O2 protein in rcl1 correlates with reduced storage protein accumulation and opaque kernel phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports maintaining translational competence for O2 in strategies aimed at improving zein accumulation; stacking O2 activation alone may fail if ribosome biogenesis is impaired
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: O2 itself was not genetically manipulated in this paper and serves as a core downstream regulatory node explaining nutrient phenotype under RCL1 deficiency.
